message:
fix(codegen): Wrap PyValue reassignments in runtime.PyValue.from()

When a variable is first assigned with PyValue type (uncertain/unknown)
and later reassigned, the RHS expression must also be wrapped in
runtime.PyValue.from() to maintain type consistency.

Changes:
- Add reassign_pyvalue_wrap flag to track PyValue reassignment needs
- Emit runtime.PyValue.from() wrapper for reassignment when declared type is pyvalue
- Make binopProducesPyValue public for use in assign.zig
- Update symbol_table with pyvalue type after first assignment wrapping

This fixes type mismatch errors like:
  var vv: runtime.PyValue = runtime.PyValue.from(v);
  vv = -v;  // Error: expected PyValue, found i64
Now correctly generates:
  vv = runtime.PyValue.from(-v);
